blocks.html_en: • The gains came in three phases: slow experimentation (2025), a tooling-driven adoption bump (October 2025), then a step-change in delegation intensity after Opus 4.6 shipped in February 2026—same engineers, same tools, but they stopped supervising every step.

sentences.en: The gains came in three phases: slow experimentation (2025), a tooling-driven adoption bump (October 2025), then a step-change in delegation intensity after Opus 4.6 shipped in February 2026—same engineers, same tools, but they stopped supervising every step.
sentences.zh: 成果分为三个阶段：缓慢的实验（2025 年）、工具驱动的采用高峰（2025 年 10 月），然后是 2026 年 2 月 Opus 4.6 发布后授权强度的阶跃变化——同样的工程师，同样的工具，但他们不再监督每一步。

blocks.html_en: • Our core thesis: AI amplifies your existing practices. It makes a dysfunctional org more dysfunctional and a high-autonomy, high-ownership org faster. The practices—continuous delivery, fast and AI-legible CI, closed-loop observability, CLAUDE.md, feature flags—are the actual story, not the multiplier.

sentences.en: Our core thesis: AI amplifies your existing practices.
sentences.zh: 我们的核心论点：人工智能放大您现有的实践。

sentences.en: It makes a dysfunctional org more dysfunctional and a high-autonomy, high-ownership org faster.
sentences.zh: 它使功能失调的组织更加功能失调，而高度自治、高所有权的组织则更快。

sentences.en: The practices—continuous delivery, fast and AI-legible CI, closed-loop observability, CLAUDE.md, feature flags—are the actual story, not the multiplier.
sentences.zh: 这些实践——持续交付、快速且人工智能可读的 CI、闭环可观察性、CLAUDE.md、功能标志——是实际的故事，而不是乘数。

blocks.html_en: Why did I put that big number in the title? It’s the number that gets you to click. But we’re going to dig into all of the caveats and details beyond just 2.5x-ing our throughput, including whether we let quality slip. This blog and its companion post are our report-out on what we did to achieve that result, how we kept the systems underneath that throughput from falling over, and what we learned in the process.

sentences.en: Why did I put that big number in the title?
sentences.zh: 为什么我在标题中放这么大的数字？

sentences.en: It’s the number that gets you to click.
sentences.zh: 这是让您点击的数字。

sentences.en: But we’re going to dig into all of the caveats and details beyond just 2.5x-ing our throughput, including whether we let quality slip.
sentences.zh: 但我们将深入研究所有注意事项和细节，而不仅仅是吞吐量的 2.5 倍，包括我们是否让质量下降。

sentences.en: This blog and its companion post are our report-out on what we did to achieve that result, how we kept the systems underneath that throughput from falling over, and what we learned in the process.
sentences.zh: 这篇博客及其配套文章是我们为实现这一结果所做的工作、我们如何保持该吞吐量下的系统不崩溃以及我们在此过程中学到的知识的报告。

blocks.html_en: Setting out to double productivity in a year

sentences.en: Setting out to double productivity in a year
sentences.zh: 计划在一年内将生产力提高一倍

blocks.html_en: In mid-August 2025, our founders sent a letter to the whole company, not just engineering: each of us should aim to double our productivity over the next year. It was addressed to individuals; the framing underneath it was a team sport, not an individual race, and nobody was supposed to read it as a contest with their teammates. The letter named Darragh Curran’s Intercom 2x post as the framing inspiration, explicitly. Eight months later, we started to measure and reflect. This post follows on from Darragh’s retrospective on hitting 2x in nine months and Kesha Mykhailov and Niamh Young’s post on safely scaling AI auto-approval. We’re smaller than Intercom and a couple of years younger, and we’ve historically followed similar paths a few months behind them.

sentences.en: In mid-August 2025, our founders sent a letter to the whole company, not just engineering: each of us should aim to double our productivity over the next year.
sentences.zh: 2025 年 8 月中旬，我们的创始人向整个公司（而不仅仅是工程部门）发出了一封信：我们每个人都应该致力于在明年将生产力提高一倍。

sentences.en: It was addressed to individuals; the framing underneath it was a team sport, not an individual race, and nobody was supposed to read it as a contest with their teammates.
sentences.zh: 它是针对个人的；它背后的框架是一项团队运动，而不是个人比赛，任何人都不应该将其视为与队友的比赛。

sentences.en: The letter named Darragh Curran’s Intercom 2x post as the framing inspiration, explicitly.
sentences.zh: 这封信明确地将 Darragh Curran 的 Intercom 2x 帖子命名为框架灵感。

sentences.en: Eight months later, we started to measure and reflect.
sentences.zh: 八个月后，我们开始衡量和反思。

sentences.en: This post follows on from Darragh’s retrospective on hitting 2x in nine months and Kesha Mykhailov and Niamh Young’s post on safely scaling AI auto-approval.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章是 Darragh 关于在九个月内实现 2 倍增长的回顾以及 Kesha Mykhailov 和 Niamh Young 关于安全扩展人工智能自动审批的帖子的后续文章。

sentences.en: We’re smaller than Intercom and a couple of years younger, and we’ve historically followed similar paths a few months behind them.
sentences.zh: 我们比 Intercom 小，而且年轻几年，而且我们历来都比 Intercom 落后几个月，走着类似的道路。

blocks.html_en: The number of merges on a peak weekday to Honeycomb’s monorepo more than doubled from about 30 in early 2025 to about 74 in April 2026. This codebase doubled in sixteen and a half months, from approximately 0.97 million lines at the end of 2024 past 1.94 million in the first week of May 2026, and it sits at 2.1 million as of early July. The previous doubling had taken nearly three years. Most of that net-new code was co-written with or reviewed by AI somewhere in its history.

sentences.en: The number of merges on a peak weekday to Honeycomb’s monorepo more than doubled from about 30 in early 2025 to about 74 in April 2026.
sentences.zh: Honeycomb 单一仓库在工作日高峰期的合并数量增加了一倍多，从 2025 年初的约 30 起增加到 2026 年 4 月的约 74 起。

sentences.en: This codebase doubled in sixteen and a half months, from approximately 0.97 million lines at the end of 2024 past 1.94 million in the first week of May 2026, and it sits at 2.1 million as of early July.
sentences.zh: 该代码库在 16 个半月内翻了一番，从 2024 年底的约 97 万行增加到 2026 年 5 月第一周的 194 万行，截至 7 月初已达到 210 万行。

sentences.en: The previous doubling had taken nearly three years.
sentences.zh: 上一次翻倍花了近三年的时间。

sentences.en: Most of that net-new code was co-written with or reviewed by AI somewhere in its history.
sentences.zh: 大多数全新代码都是在其历史上的某个地方与人工智能共同编写或审查的。

blocks.html_en: Bee-ing honest about that number

sentences.en: Bee-ing honest about that number
sentences.zh: 诚实地对待这个数字

blocks.html_en: • It’s peak weekday, not calendar average. Wednesday no-meeting days are the most productive, both before and after AI; calendar average is roughly half of peak. That’s the peak. Don’t go looking for 70 PRs on a random Tuesday and conclude I lied to you.

sentences.en: It’s peak weekday, not calendar average.
sentences.zh: 这是工作日的高峰期，而不是日历平均值。

sentences.en: Wednesday no-meeting days are the most productive, both before and after AI; calendar average is roughly half of peak.
sentences.zh: 无论是在人工智能之前还是之后，周三不开会的日子都是最有成效的；日历平均值大约是峰值的一半。

sentences.en: That’s the peak.
sentences.zh: 这就是巅峰。

sentences.en: Don’t go looking for 70 PRs on a random Tuesday and conclude I lied to you.
sentences.zh: 不要在周二随机查找 70 个 PR 并得出结论我对你撒了谎。

blocks.html_en: • It’s a floor, not a true count. One of our heaviest Claude Code users, by token volume, has zero AI-attributed git commits. They ran 22 sessions and 199 million tokens through Claude Code in 30 days, and git saw none of it, because their Co-Authored-By trailer is disabled at the tool level. If we can’t measure their AI usage, we can’t measure several other people’s either.

sentences.en: It’s a floor, not a true count.
sentences.zh: 这是一个楼层，而不是真正的计数。

sentences.en: One of our heaviest Claude Code users, by token volume, has zero AI-attributed git commits.
sentences.zh: 按代币量计算，我们最重的 Claude Code 用户之一的 AI 归因 git 提交为零。

sentences.en: They ran 22 sessions and 199 million tokens through Claude Code in 30 days, and git saw none of it, because their Co-Authored-By trailer is disabled at the tool level.
sentences.zh: 他们在 30 天内通过 Claude Code 运行了 22 个会话和 1.99 亿个代币，而 git 没有看到任何结果，因为他们的 Co-Authored-By 预告片在工具级别被禁用。

sentences.en: If we can’t measure their AI usage, we can’t measure several other people’s either.
sentences.zh: 如果我们无法衡量他们的人工智能使用情况，我们也无法衡量其他人的人工智能使用情况。

blocks.html_en: • It’s entangled with org changes happening at the same time. In early January 2026, our founders sent a follow-up letter naming sharper strategic stakes than the August one had: rebuilding Honeycomb’s product surface and market posture to be AI-first, well beyond the original productivity target. The mid-January realignment toward greenfield, higher-AI-leverage work followed from that letter. We onboarded new engineers. We invested in platform engineering. Anyone who tells you a single factor caused their team’s gain is overstating it, including us.

sentences.en: It’s entangled with org changes happening at the same time.
sentences.zh: 它与同时发生的组织变革纠缠在一起。

sentences.en: In early January 2026, our founders sent a follow-up letter naming sharper strategic stakes than the August one had: rebuilding Honeycomb’s product surface and market posture to be AI-first, well beyond the original productivity target.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 1 月上旬，我们的创始人发送了一封后续信函，指出了比 8 月份的信函更明确的战略利益：重建 Honeycomb 的产品面和市场态势，以人工智能为先，远远超出最初的生产力目标。

sentences.en: The mid-January realignment toward greenfield, higher-AI-leverage work followed from that letter.
sentences.zh: 继这封信之后，一月中旬的调整转向了绿地、更高人工智能杠杆的工作。

sentences.en: We onboarded new engineers.
sentences.zh: 我们聘请了新工程师。

sentences.en: We invested in platform engineering.
sentences.zh: 我们投资了平台工程。

sentences.en: Anyone who tells you a single factor caused their team’s gain is overstating it, including us.
sentences.zh: 任何人如果告诉你一个单一因素导致了他们团队的进步，那就夸大了，包括我们。

blocks.html_en: There’s a big leap from “AI tooling makes engineering teams genuinely faster” to “any team that adopts it will get the same result.” Most of this post lives in the gap between those two claims. The interesting story isn’t the multiplier; it’s what we did to keep things stable underneath it, what it cost us, and what we’re still figuring out. The short version, and the line I keep coming back to every time I’m invited on stage: AI amplifies your existing practices. It can make a dysfunctional org more dysfunctional, or it can bring out the best in an org that already has high autonomy, ownership, and feedback loops. We weren’t setting out to prove that thesis; we took a challenge, and the thesis became visible after the fact.

sentences.en: There’s a big leap from “AI tooling makes engineering teams genuinely faster” to “any team that adopts it will get the same result.”
sentences.zh: 从“人工智能工具让工程团队真正更快”到“任何采用它的团队都会得到相同的结果”，这是一个巨大的飞跃。

sentences.en: Most of this post lives in the gap between those two claims.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章的大部分内容都存在于这两种说法之间。

sentences.en: The interesting story isn’t the multiplier; it’s what we did to keep things stable underneath it, what it cost us, and what we’re still figuring out.
sentences.zh: 有趣的故事不在于乘数，而在于乘数。这是我们为保持稳定而所做的事情，我们为此付出的代价，以及我们仍在弄清楚的事情。

sentences.en: The short version, and the line I keep coming back to every time I’m invited on stage: AI amplifies your existing practices.
sentences.zh: 简短的版本，以及每次我受邀上台时我都会重复的一句话：人工智能放大了你现有的实践。

sentences.en: It can make a dysfunctional org more dysfunctional, or it can bring out the best in an org that already has high autonomy, ownership, and feedback loops.
sentences.zh: 它可以让一个功能失调的组织变得更加功能失调，或者它可以让一个已经拥有高度自主权、所有权和反馈循环的组织发挥出最好的一面。

sentences.en: We weren’t setting out to prove that thesis; we took a challenge, and the thesis became visible after the fact.
sentences.zh: 我们并不是要证明这个论点；而是要证明这个论点。我们接受了挑战，论文在事后变得可见。

blocks.html_en: If you caught my “AI is like chocolate” talk, you know the bit: chocolate doesn’t belong on everything, too much of it in one sitting will make you sick, and no amount of chocolate substitutes for knowing how to cook. I gave that talk as a pessimist turned realist, and that’s still who I am. What changed between then and now isn’t the metaphor; it’s that the tooling and the practices around it got good enough that chocolate’s rightful place in the kitchen got bigger. It’s more versatile and forgiving than it used to be. The concessions later in this post, in “Where the skeptics are right,” are concessions I’m still making. Being a reformed skeptic doesn’t mean I stopped being one.

sentences.en: If you caught my “AI is like chocolate” talk, you know the bit: chocolate doesn’t belong on everything, too much of it in one sitting will make you sick, and no amount of chocolate substitutes for knowing how to cook.
sentences.zh: 如果你听过我的“人工智能就像巧克力”的演讲，你就会知道这一点：巧克力并不属于所有东西，一次吃太多会让你生病，而且再多的巧克力也无法替代你学会如何烹饪。

sentences.en: I gave that talk as a pessimist turned realist, and that’s still who I am.
sentences.zh: 我以一个悲观主义者转变为现实主义者的身份发表了这次演讲，而我仍然是这样的。

sentences.en: What changed between then and now isn’t the metaphor; it’s that the tooling and the practices around it got good enough that chocolate’s rightful place in the kitchen got bigger.
sentences.zh: 从那时到现在，改变的不是隐喻，而是隐喻。正是因为工具和相关实践变得足够好，巧克力在厨房中的应有地位才变得更大。

sentences.en: It’s more versatile and forgiving than it used to be.
sentences.zh: 它比以前更加通用和宽容。

sentences.en: The concessions later in this post, in “Where the skeptics are right,” are concessions I’m still making.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章后面的“怀疑论者是对的”中的让步是我仍在做出的让步。

sentences.en: Being a reformed skeptic doesn’t mean I stopped being one.
sentences.zh: 成为一名改革后的怀疑论者并不意味着我不再是怀疑论者。

blocks.html_en: You should take every number on this page, including ours, with a grain of salt. The methodology matters more than the magnitude. Keep your semantic bullshit defenses up against hype and plausible-sounding data, all the way through the rest of this post.

sentences.en: You should take every number on this page, including ours, with a grain of salt.
sentences.zh: 您应该对本页上的每个数字（包括我们的数字）持保留态度。

sentences.en: The methodology matters more than the magnitude.
sentences.zh: 方法论比规模更重要。

sentences.en: Keep your semantic bullshit defenses up against hype and plausible-sounding data, all the way through the rest of this post.
sentences.zh: 在这篇文章的其余部分中，保持你的语义废话防御免受炒作和听起来合理的数据的影响。

blocks.html_en: What the numbers do and don’t say about quality

sentences.en: What the numbers do and don’t say about quality
sentences.zh: 数字对质量的影响和不影响

blocks.html_en: We haven’t had a spectacular AI-caused failure. No “AI deleted my database,” no “AI shipped code that corrupted user data.” That’s not luck; it’s not new either. It’s a result of designing defensively, whether the chaos agents be human or robot. Stacking agents on top of existing infrastructure with bulkheads between components, reviews, deploy trains, feature flags, and least-privilege access has meant outages are lower-impact, rather than either non-existent or uniformly critical-severity. If you don’t have that in place yet, that’s the thing to fix before you scale up AI usage, not after. A dropped database is a systems design issue: somebody skipped building the guardrail that would have caught it, regardless of who or what wrote the code.

sentences.en: We haven’t had a spectacular AI-caused failure.
sentences.zh: 我们还没有遇到过由人工智能引起的重大失败。

sentences.en: No “AI deleted my database,” no “AI shipped code that corrupted user data.”
sentences.zh: 没有“人工智能删除了我的数据库”，没有“人工智能发送了损坏用户数据的代码”。

sentences.en: That’s not luck; it’s not new either.
sentences.zh: 这不是运气，而是运气。这也不是什么新鲜事。

sentences.en: It’s a result of designing defensively, whether the chaos agents be human or robot.
sentences.zh: 这是防御性设计的结果，无论混沌代理是人类还是机器人。

sentences.en: Stacking agents on top of existing infrastructure with bulkheads between components, reviews, deploy trains, feature flags, and least-privilege access has meant outages are lower-impact, rather than either non-existent or uniformly critical-severity.
sentences.zh: 将代理堆叠在现有基础设施之上，并在组件、审查、部署列车、功能标志和最低权限访问之间设置隔板，这意味着中断的影响较小，而不是不存在或统一的严重程度。

sentences.en: If you don’t have that in place yet, that’s the thing to fix before you scale up AI usage, not after.
sentences.zh: 如果你还没有做到这一点，那么这就是在扩大人工智能使用之前而不是之后需要解决的问题。

sentences.en: A dropped database is a systems design issue: somebody skipped building the guardrail that would have caught it, regardless of who or what wrote the code.
sentences.zh: 丢失的数据库是一个系统设计问题：有人跳过了构建可以捕获它的护栏，无论是谁或什么编写了代码。

blocks.html_en: Incidents are growing in absolute count. From a 2024 baseline of about 18.5 incidents per quarter, Q1 2026 hit 32, 1.7x baseline, against PR throughput at about 2.5x baseline; for one quarter that looked sub-linear. Q2 didn’t hold: 53 incidents, 2.9x baseline, even as PR throughput itself held roughly flat once you back out the May freeze weeks and the January ramp-up swarm. Two quarters in, incident growth tracks change volume about as linearly as you’d expect. Change is the leading driver of incidents industry-wide (see the VOID report, Google’s DORA research), and we’re shipping a lot more of it. The law of large numbers caught up with us.

sentences.en: Incidents are growing in absolute count.
sentences.zh: 事故的绝对数量正在增加。

sentences.en: From a 2024 baseline of about 18.5 incidents per quarter, Q1 2026 hit 32, 1.7x baseline, against PR throughput at about 2.5x baseline; for one quarter that looked sub-linear.
sentences.zh: 从 2024 年每季度约 18.5 起事件的基线来看，2026 年第一季度达到了 32 起，即基线的 1.7 倍，而 PR 吞吐量约为基线的 2.5 倍；有四分之一的时间看起来不太线性。

sentences.en: Q2 didn’t hold: 53 incidents, 2.9x baseline, even as PR throughput itself held roughly flat once you back out the May freeze weeks and the January ramp-up swarm.
sentences.zh: 第二季度的情况并不成立：发生 53 起事件，是基线的 2.9 倍，尽管一旦退出 5 月份的冻结周和 1 月份的高峰期，公关吞吐量本身就保持大致持平。

sentences.en: Two quarters in, incident growth tracks change volume about as linearly as you’d expect.
sentences.zh: 两个季度后，事件增长与变化量的关系与您预期的呈线性关系。

sentences.en: Change is the leading driver of incidents industry-wide (see the VOID report, Google’s DORA research), and we’re shipping a lot more of it.
sentences.zh: 变革是全行业事件的主要驱动因素（请参阅 VOID 报告、Google 的 DORA 研究），而且我们正在交付更多这样的变革。

sentences.en: The law of large numbers caught up with us.
sentences.zh: 大数定律追上了我们。

blocks.html_en: What the data actually supports is two things: the absence of spectacular failures, plus some integrated second-order effects we’re picking up on. Broader AI-causation narratives tend to be self-flattering, whichever direction they point; AI is woven in deeply enough at this point that isolating it as a single cause is rarely a meaningful exercise. Attribution-by-cause is a fairy tale we humans tell ourselves to feel better about whatever stance we already hold.

sentences.en: What the data actually supports is two things: the absence of spectacular failures, plus some integrated second-order effects we’re picking up on.
sentences.zh: 数据实际上支持两件事：没有发生重大故障，再加上我们正在发现的一些综合二阶效应。

sentences.en: Broader AI-causation narratives tend to be self-flattering, whichever direction they point; AI is woven in deeply enough at this point that isolating it as a single cause is rarely a meaningful exercise.
sentences.zh: 更广泛的人工智能因果关系叙述往往是自我奉承，无论它们指向哪个方向；人工智能在这一点上已经融入得足够深，将其孤立为单一原因很少是有意义的做法。

sentences.en: Attribution-by-cause is a fairy tale we humans tell ourselves to feel better about whatever stance we already hold.
sentences.zh: 因果归因是一个童话故事，我们人类告诉自己，无论我们已经持有什么立场，都要感觉更好。

blocks.html_en: More changes shipped means more chances for a defect to land somewhere in the batch, at whatever the org’s baseline defect rate happens to be. We’re shipping far more change, so we get far more incidents, in roughly the proportion you’d expect. It’s too early to say whether AI assistance in debugging reduces incident severity once something breaks; in some cases it’s helped us find the root cause fast, in others it’s sent us chasing a confident, wrong answer instead. That volume showed up as real strain on the teams absorbing it, not just as a line going up on a chart. We’re leaning on better automated preflight checks, among other levers, to try to bend that curve back down. The burnout risk that comes with capturing AI’s speed as pure output rather than sustainable pace is a real one, and worth naming rather than assuming away.

sentences.en: More changes shipped means more chances for a defect to land somewhere in the batch, at whatever the org’s baseline defect rate happens to be.
sentences.zh: 交付的变更越多，意味着缺陷出现在批次中某个位置的机会就越大，无论组织的基准缺陷率是多少。

sentences.en: We’re shipping far more change, so we get far more incidents, in roughly the proportion you’d expect.
sentences.zh: 我们正在运送更多的零钱，因此我们会遇到更多的事件，大致与您预期的比例相同。

sentences.en: It’s too early to say whether AI assistance in debugging reduces incident severity once something breaks; in some cases it’s helped us find the root cause fast, in others it’s sent us chasing a confident, wrong answer instead.
sentences.zh: 一旦出现问题，现在判断人工智能在调试方面的协助是否会降低事件的严重性还为时过早。在某些情况下，它帮助我们快速找到根本原因，而在另一些情况下，它却让我们去寻找一个自信的、错误的答案。

sentences.en: That volume showed up as real strain on the teams absorbing it, not just as a line going up on a chart.
sentences.zh: 这个交易量显示出对吸收它的团队的真正压力，而不仅仅是图表上一条上升的线。

sentences.en: We’re leaning on better automated preflight checks, among other levers, to try to bend that curve back down.
sentences.zh: 我们依靠更好的自动化飞行前检查以及其他手段来尝试将曲线向下弯曲。

sentences.en: The burnout risk that comes with capturing AI’s speed as pure output rather than sustainable pace is a real one, and worth naming rather than assuming away.
sentences.zh: 将人工智能的速度视为纯粹的产出而不是可持续的速度所带来的倦怠风险是真实存在的，值得一提而不是假设。

blocks.html_en: The metric I’d rather put on the wall isn’t PRs per day. Throughput is an input metric, not the product; it’s just the coarse measure we have today to demonstrate step-change. Throughput going up while user outcomes plateau or decline is, by definition, enshittification.

sentences.en: The metric I’d rather put on the wall isn’t PRs per day.
sentences.zh: 我宁愿挂在墙上的指标不是每天的 PR。

sentences.en: Throughput is an input metric, not the product; it’s just the coarse measure we have today to demonstrate step-change.
sentences.zh: 吞吐量是一个输入指标，而不是产品；这只是我们今天用来展示逐步变化的粗略衡量标准。

sentences.en: Throughput going up while user outcomes plateau or decline is, by definition, enshittification.
sentences.zh: 根据定义，当用户结果停滞或下降时，吞吐量会上升，这就是enshitification。

blocks.html_en: What does “AI contribution” actually mean?

sentences.en: What does “AI contribution” actually mean?
sentences.zh: “人工智能贡献”到底意味着什么？

blocks.html_en: There’s no single “AI percentage.” There are at least four different denominators, and you have to be precise about which question you’re asking before you quote a number at anyone.

sentences.en: There’s no single “AI percentage.”
sentences.zh: 没有单一的“人工智能百分比”。

sentences.en: There are at least four different denominators, and you have to be precise about which question you’re asking before you quote a number at anyone.
sentences.zh: 至少有四种不同的分母，在向任何人引用数字之前，你必须准确地确定你要问的是哪个问题。

blocks.html_en: (Vendor dashboards will happily hand you an “AI-influenced PRs” number with a confidence toggle. Set to loose confidence, ours cheerfully reported a majority of PRs as AI before we’d done any of the work below. Don’t trust low confidence. The numbers in this post come from our own git history and telemetry, calibrated by hand.)

sentences.en: (Vendor dashboards will happily hand you an “AI-influenced PRs” number with a confidence toggle.
sentences.zh: （供应商仪表板会很乐意为您提供一个带有信心开关的“受人工智能影响的 PR”号码。

sentences.en: Set to loose confidence, ours cheerfully reported a majority of PRs as AI before we’d done any of the work below.
sentences.zh: 我们失去了信心，在完成以下任何工作之前，我们高兴地将大部分 PR 报告为 AI。

sentences.en: Don’t trust low confidence.
sentences.zh: 不要相信信心不足的人。

sentences.en: The numbers in this post come from our own git history and telemetry, calibrated by hand.)
sentences.zh: 这篇文章中的数字来自我们自己的 git 历史记录和遥测数据，经过手工校准。）

blocks.html_en: These are calibrated floors, not point estimates. We got there by layering three corrections onto git’s raw signal: local branch trailers (245 PRs whose AI attribution was stripped by squash-merge), GitHub-API branch trailers (117 PRs where branches were deleted post-merge but we recovered the trailers via GraphQL across all 7,952 PRs), and a smell-test telemetry override (112 PRs from engineers with zero git AI attribution but heavy Claude Code session telemetry, gated per month against their actual session activity).

sentences.en: These are calibrated floors, not point estimates.
sentences.zh: 这些是校准的下限，而不是点估计。

sentences.en: We got there by layering three corrections onto git’s raw signal: local branch trailers (245 PRs whose AI attribution was stripped by squash-merge), GitHub-API branch trailers (117 PRs where branches were deleted post-merge but we recovered the trailers via GraphQL across all 7,952 PRs), and a smell-test telemetry override (112 PRs from engineers with zero git AI attribution but heavy Claude Code session telemetry, gated per month against their actual session activity).
sentences.zh: 我们通过对 git 的原始信号进行三项更正来实现这一目标：本地分支预告片（245 个 PR，其 AI 属性被挤压合并剥离）、GitHub-API 分支预告片（117 个 PR，其中分支在合并后被删除，但我们通过 GraphQL 在所有 7,952 个 PR 中恢复了预告片），以及气味测试遥测覆盖（来自 git AI 属性为零但 Claude 代码会话较多的工程师的 112 个 PR）遥测，每月针对其实际会话活动进行门控）。

blocks.html_en: The 95% engineer-level adoption figure, which is closer to our gut estimates, reconciles naturally with the lower PR-level (63%) and line-level (75%) floors: high adoption, selective per-PR use. We can measure floors from git history. We can’t measure ceilings. Being clear about the difference matters more than the specific numbers do.

sentences.en: The 95% engineer-level adoption figure, which is closer to our gut estimates, reconciles naturally with the lower PR-level (63%) and line-level (75%) floors: high adoption, selective per-PR use.
sentences.zh: 95% 的工程师级采用率数字更接近我们的直觉估计，与较低的 PR 级别 (63%) 和生产线级 (75%) 下限自然一致：采用率高，每个 PR 有选择性使用。

sentences.en: We can measure floors from git history.
sentences.zh: 我们可以从 git 历史记录中测量楼层。

sentences.en: We can’t measure ceilings.
sentences.zh: 我们无法测量天花板。

sentences.en: Being clear about the difference matters more than the specific numbers do.
sentences.zh: 明确差异比具体数字更重要。

blocks.html_en: Surviving lines at HEAD since 2024. The red band is a floor; lines from PRs with AI attribution somewhere in their history. The blue band is “no attribution,” not “no AI.”

sentences.en: Surviving lines at HEAD since 2024.
sentences.zh: 自 2024 年以来，HEAD 的剩余线路。

sentences.en: The red band is a floor; lines from PRs with AI attribution somewhere in their history.
sentences.zh: 红色带是地板；历史上某处带有 AI 归因的公关台词。

sentences.en: The blue band is “no attribution,” not “no AI.”
sentences.zh: 蓝色带是“无归因”，而不是“无人工智能”。

blocks.html_en: What happened

sentences.en: What happened
sentences.zh: 发生了什么

blocks.html_en: The adoption curve at Honeycomb has three distinct phases. Each one was driven by something different.

sentences.en: The adoption curve at Honeycomb has three distinct phases.
sentences.zh: Honeycomb 的采用曲线分为三个不同的阶段。

sentences.en: Each one was driven by something different.
sentences.zh: 每个人都是由不同的东西驱动的。

blocks.html_en: The first phase, April through September 2025, was sustained low-rate experimentation. About one or two new engineers adopted Claude Code per month, with support but no mandate; engineers were exploring on their own, at least until mid-August, when the founders’ letter landed and the picture blurs. AI’s share of new lines climbed modestly, peaked around 18% in June, then drifted back down to 8% by October as the honeymoon wore off. This is what “leadership opened the door” looks like in practice: not a discrete push so much as a sustained green light. The dip in the second half of 2025 was evaluation, not failure: engineers tried Claude with the model available at the time, decided it didn’t yet justify the friction, and pulled back. If the catnip is rotten, herding cats to eat the catnip is even more difficult.

sentences.en: The first phase, April through September 2025, was sustained low-rate experimentation.
sentences.zh: 第一阶段是 2025 年 4 月至 9 月，持续进行低速率实验。

sentences.en: About one or two new engineers adopted Claude Code per month, with support but no mandate; engineers were exploring on their own, at least until mid-August, when the founders’ letter landed and the picture blurs.
sentences.zh: 每月大约有一到两名新工程师采用 Claude Code，有支持但没有强制要求；工程师们一直在自己探索，至少直到 8 月中旬，当创始人的信到达时，情况变得模糊起来。

sentences.en: AI’s share of new lines climbed modestly, peaked around 18% in June, then drifted back down to 8% by October as the honeymoon wore off.
sentences.zh: 人工智能在新线路中的份额小幅攀升，在 6 月份达到 18% 左右的峰值，然后随着蜜月期的结束，到 10 月份回落至 8%。

sentences.en: This is what “leadership opened the door” looks like in practice: not a discrete push so much as a sustained green light.
sentences.zh: 这就是“领导打开大门”在实践中的样子：与其说是一次离散的推动，不如说是持续的绿灯。

sentences.en: The dip in the second half of 2025 was evaluation, not failure: engineers tried Claude with the model available at the time, decided it didn’t yet justify the friction, and pulled back.
sentences.zh: 2025 年下半年的下降是评估，而不是失败：工程师尝试使用 Claude 当时可用的模型，认为它还不能证明摩擦的合理性，然后撤回。

sentences.en: If the catnip is rotten, herding cats to eat the catnip is even more difficult.
sentences.zh: 如果猫薄荷腐烂了，放牧猫吃猫薄荷就更困难了。

blocks.html_en: The second phase began in October 2025 with a Claude Code harness improvement. Seven new adopters that month, with no model release behind it; tooling quality alone moved the needle. November and December were a pause (although some engineers used the holiday break to try the tools in their personal capacities). Then Claude 4.5 landed in late December, and adoption picked back up in January 2026 with eight new adopters, and AI’s share of new lines climbing to 18% by January.

sentences.en: The second phase began in October 2025 with a Claude Code harness improvement.
sentences.zh: 第二阶段于 2025 年 10 月开始，对 Claude Code 安全带进行了改进。

sentences.en: Seven new adopters that month, with no model release behind it; tooling quality alone moved the needle.
sentences.zh: 当月有 7 位新采用者，但没有发布任何型号；仅工具质量就起到了推动作用。

sentences.en: November and December were a pause (although some engineers used the holiday break to try the tools in their personal capacities).
sentences.zh: 11 月和 12 月是一个暂停期（尽管一些工程师利用假期以个人身份尝试这些工具）。

sentences.en: Then Claude 4.5 landed in late December, and adoption picked back up in January 2026 with eight new adopters, and AI’s share of new lines climbing to 18% by January.
sentences.zh: 然后 Claude 4.5 于 12 月底登陆，采用率在 2026 年 1 月回升，有 8 个新采用者，到 1 月份，AI 在新线路中的份额攀升至 18%。

blocks.html_en: Engineers with at least one AI-attributed commit, cumulative. Each step change lines up with a capability event, not a flat schedule.

sentences.en: Engineers with at least one AI-attributed commit, cumulative.
sentences.zh: 至少有一项 AI 贡献的工程师，累计。

sentences.en: Each step change lines up with a capability event, not a flat schedule.
sentences.zh: 每个步骤的变更都与功能事件相关，而不是固定的时间表。

blocks.html_en: Opus 4.6 launched on Thursday, February 5, 2026. The session-rate takeoff in our Claude Code telemetry lands on February 11-13: the first full work-week after a Thursday launch with a Friday-and-weekend bake-in. Distinct monthly Claude Code users stayed nearly flat across January, February, and March (59, 63, 64). Sessions per user 3.3x’d over the same window (21, 35, 70). AI’s share of new lines went from 18% in January to 46% in February to 65% in March.

sentences.en: Opus 4.6 launched on Thursday, February 5, 2026.
sentences.zh: Opus 4.6 于 2026 年 2 月 5 日星期四发布。

sentences.en: The session-rate takeoff in our Claude Code telemetry lands on February 11-13: the first full work-week after a Thursday launch with a Friday-and-weekend bake-in.
sentences.zh: 我们的 Claude Code 遥测技术的会话率起飞时间为 2 月 11 日至 13 日：周四发布并进行周五和周末预演后的第一个完整工作周。

sentences.en: Distinct monthly Claude Code users stayed nearly flat across January, February, and March (59, 63, 64).
sentences.zh: 1 月、2 月和 3 月，不同的月度 Claude Code 用户几乎持平（59、63、64）。

sentences.en: Sessions per user 3.3x’d over the same window (21, 35, 70).
sentences.zh: 同一窗口内每个用户的会话数是原来的 3.3 倍（21、35、70）。

sentences.en: AI’s share of new lines went from 18% in January to 46% in February to 65% in March.
sentences.zh: AI新增线路的份额从1月份的18%上升到2月份的46%，再到3月份的65%。

blocks.html_en: We’ve been calling this confidence to delegate. The same engineers, with the same tooling, with access to the same model family, simply changed how they used it. They stopped consulting or closely monitoring each step, and started delegating. The lift is per-user-intensity, not headcount enabled.

sentences.en: We’ve been calling this confidence to delegate.
sentences.zh: 我们一直把这种信心称为授权。

sentences.en: The same engineers, with the same tooling, with access to the same model family, simply changed how they used it.
sentences.zh: 相同的工程师，使用相同的工具，可以访问相同的模型系列，只是改变了他们的使用方式。

sentences.en: They stopped consulting or closely monitoring each step, and started delegating.
sentences.zh: 他们停止咨询或密切监控每一步，并开始授权。

sentences.en: The lift is per-user-intensity, not headcount enabled.
sentences.zh: 提升是按用户强度计算的，而不是按人数计算。

blocks.html_en: The Q1 proof, one magnitude per panel: engineer count barely moved, sessions per engineer exploded, and committed PRs-by-model shows the delegation landing on Opus 4.6 specifically.

sentences.en: The Q1 proof, one magnitude per panel: engineer count barely moved, sessions per engineer exploded, and committed PRs-by-model shows the delegation landing on Opus 4.6 specifically.
sentences.zh: 第一季度的证明，每个面板一个量级：工程师数量几乎没有变化，每个工程师的会话数量激增，并且按模型提交的 PR 显示代表团具体登陆了 Opus 4.6。

blocks.html_en: While the median engineer’s PR throughput grew about 45% relative to its pre-February baseline (call that baseline 1.0x), the top of the distribution moved much further. P75 went from about 1.8x baseline to about 2.9x, roughly +64% in relative terms; the weekly maximum across our active engineers went from a 3.2x-6.4x baseline range pre-February to a 7.7x-12.3x range in April. The floor barely moved at all (P25 went from about 0.5x baseline to about 0.8x). The AI uplift is concentrated at the top end of the distribution. It is not a universal rising tide that automatically boosts every engineer, and that’s okay, because not all engineering is in the bucket of things AI accelerates. As Charity says, the closer to touching bytes on disk you are, the more cautious you need to be about reviewing everything with a paranoid lens.

sentences.en: While the median engineer’s PR throughput grew about 45% relative to its pre-February baseline (call that baseline 1.0x), the top of the distribution moved much further.
sentences.zh: 虽然工程师的 PR 吞吐量中值相对于 2 月前的基线（称为基线 1.0 倍）增长了约 45%，但分布的顶部移动得更远。

sentences.en: P75 went from about 1.8x baseline to about 2.9x, roughly +64% in relative terms; the weekly maximum across our active engineers went from a 3.2x-6.4x baseline range pre-February to a 7.7x-12.3x range in April.
sentences.zh: P75 从约 1.8 倍基线增至约 2.9 倍，相对而言大约+64%；我们活跃工程师的每周最高收益从 2 月前的 3.2 倍 - 6.4 倍基线范围上升到 4 月份的 7.7 倍 - 12.3 倍范围。

sentences.en: The floor barely moved at all (P25 went from about 0.5x baseline to about 0.8x).
sentences.zh: 地板几乎没有移动（P25 从约 0.5 倍基线变为约 0.8 倍）。

sentences.en: The AI uplift is concentrated at the top end of the distribution.
sentences.zh: 人工智能的提升集中在分布的顶端。

sentences.en: It is not a universal rising tide that automatically boosts every engineer, and that’s okay, because not all engineering is in the bucket of things AI accelerates.
sentences.zh: 这并不是一种普遍的上涨浪潮，自动提升每个工程师的水平，但这没关系，因为并非所有工程都属于人工智能加速的范围。

sentences.en: As Charity says, the closer to touching bytes on disk you are, the more cautious you need to be about reviewing everything with a paranoid lens.
sentences.zh: 正如查里蒂所说，你越接近接触磁盘上的字节，你就越需要谨慎地用偏执的眼光来审查一切。

blocks.html_en: We’d never had a dozen engineers a week shipping 7+ PRs each in our entire 10-year history, until March 2026. Pre-February, two to four engineers a week hit seven or more PRs; in February that was three to seven, in March seven to twelve, in April eight to sixteen. New, and now routine. And the engineers at the top aren’t a stable cast; the March-selected and April-selected top-twelve cohorts only overlap by about half. It’s a rotating cast riding the new ceiling, not a handful of superusers carrying everyone else.

sentences.en: We’d never had a dozen engineers a week shipping 7+ PRs each in our entire 10-year history, until March 2026.
sentences.zh: 在 2026 年 3 月之前，在我们整个 10 年的历史中，从来没有十几个工程师每周交付 7 个以上的 PR。

sentences.en: Pre-February, two to four engineers a week hit seven or more PRs; in February that was three to seven, in March seven to twelve, in April eight to sixteen.
sentences.zh: 2 月之前，每周有两到四名工程师达成 7 个或更多 PR；二月是三点到七点，三月是七点到十二点，四月是八点到十六点。

sentences.en: New, and now routine.
sentences.zh: 新的，现在是例行公事。

sentences.en: And the engineers at the top aren’t a stable cast; the March-selected and April-selected top-twelve cohorts only overlap by about half.
sentences.zh: 而且高层工程师的阵容也不稳定。 3 月份选出的和 4 月份选出的前十二名队列仅重叠了大约一半。

sentences.en: It’s a rotating cast riding the new ceiling, not a handful of superusers carrying everyone else.
sentences.zh: 这是一个轮流乘坐新天花板的演员，而不是少数超级用户承载其他人。

blocks.html_en: Weekly merged PRs (total/AI-attributed/no-attribution) and the per-engineer weekly distribution for each cut, September 2025 through the week of June 22. The top tail past 20 PRs per engineer-week appears in March and persists; the May dent is the freeze weeks, not decay. Bots excluded, including the autobot.

sentences.en: Weekly merged PRs (total/AI-attributed/no-attribution) and the per-engineer weekly distribution for each cut, September 2025 through the week of June 22.
sentences.zh: 2025 年 9 月至 6 月 22 日当周的每周合并 PR（总计/AI 归因/无归因）以及每次削减的每位工程师的每周分布。

sentences.en: The top tail past 20 PRs per engineer-week appears in March and persists; the May dent is the freeze weeks, not decay.
sentences.zh: 每个工程师周超过 20 个 PR 的顶尾出现在 3 月份并持续存在；五月的凹痕是冻结周，而不是腐烂。

sentences.en: Bots excluded, including the autobot.
sentences.zh: 机器人除外，包括汽车人。

blocks.html_en: Peak-weekday non-AI merges held roughly steady at 25-30 across the entire window; humans didn’t slow down on their best days to make room for AI. But at the org-wide weekly level, non-AI commit-hash volume declined notably, from about 120 PR commits a week pre-February to about 60-80 a week through February to April, while AI commit-hash volume grew from near-zero to 150-200 a week.

sentences.en: Peak-weekday non-AI merges held roughly steady at 25-30 across the entire window; humans didn’t slow down on their best days to make room for AI.
sentences.zh: 整个窗口期内，工作日非 AI 合并高峰期大致稳定在 25-30 起；人类并没有在最好的日子放慢脚步，为人工智能腾出空间。

sentences.en: But at the org-wide weekly level, non-AI commit-hash volume declined notably, from about 120 PR commits a week pre-February to about 60-80 a week through February to April, while AI commit-hash volume grew from near-zero to 150-200 a week.
sentences.zh: 但在整个组织范围内的每周水平上，非 AI 提交哈希量显着下降，从 2 月前的每周约 120 个 PR 提交减少到 2 月到 4 月的每周约 60-80 个，而 AI 提交哈希量从接近零增长到每周 150-200 个。

blocks.html_en: So engineers didn’t slow down on the days they were shipping; they shipped fewer human-attributed PRs in aggregate while shipping many more AI-attributed ones. Some of the +44 peak-weekday delta is genuinely new capacity. Some of it is substitution, where work that used to be human-attributed is now agent-attributed because the same engineers shifted to driving with AI rather than typing by hand. We can’t cleanly separate lift from substitution without a controlled experiment we don’t have.

sentences.en: So engineers didn’t slow down on the days they were shipping; they shipped fewer human-attributed PRs in aggregate while shipping many more AI-attributed ones.
sentences.zh: 因此，工程师们在交付的日子里并没有放慢脚步；他们总共发布了较少的人工公关，而发布了更多的人工智能公关。

sentences.en: Some of the +44 peak-weekday delta is genuinely new capacity.
sentences.zh: +44 高峰工作日增量中的一些确实是新容量。

sentences.en: Some of it is substitution, where work that used to be human-attributed is now agent-attributed because the same engineers shifted to driving with AI rather than typing by hand.
sentences.zh: 其中一些是替代，过去由人类负责的工作现在由代理负责，因为同样的工程师转向使用人工智能驾驶而不是用手打字。

sentences.en: We can’t cleanly separate lift from substitution without a controlled experiment we don’t have.
sentences.zh: 如果没有我们没有的对照实验，我们就无法清楚地区分提升和替代。

blocks.html_en: May, June, and a third workflow

sentences.en: May, June, and a third workflow
sentences.zh: 五月、六月和第三个工作流程

blocks.html_en: For this blog, we pulled a fresh data cut through June 28, rather than waiting the six months we’d originally planned since the May and June presentations. One methodology note before the numbers: this refresh also excludes mechanical bots (e.g. Dependabot) from every throughput denominator, something the April numbers above didn’t do. So the figures in this section aren’t a perfectly clean continuation of the ones above them. Same discipline as the rest of this post: check the methodology before you trust the magnitude.

sentences.en: For this blog, we pulled a fresh data cut through June 28, rather than waiting the six months we’d originally planned since the May and June presentations.
sentences.zh: 在本博客中，我们提取了截至 6 月 28 日的最新数据，而不是等待自 5 月和 6 月的演示以来我们最初计划的六个月。

sentences.en: One methodology note before the numbers: this refresh also excludes mechanical bots (e.g. Dependabot) from every throughput denominator, something the April numbers above didn’t do.
sentences.zh: 在数字之前注意一个方法论：这次更新还从每个吞吐量分母中排除了机械机器人（例如 Dependabot），这是上面 4 月份的数字没有做到的。

sentences.en: So the figures in this section aren’t a perfectly clean continuation of the ones above them.
sentences.zh: 因此，本节中的图表并不是上面图表的完美延续。

sentences.en: Same discipline as the rest of this post: check the methodology before you trust the magnitude.
sentences.zh: 与本文其余部分相同的原则：在相信其规模之前检查方法。

blocks.html_en: On that basis, weekday-average merges went 38.0 in March, 47.9 in April, then dipped to 36.0 in May before climbing back to 41.9 across the four complete weeks of June. The May dip isn’t engineers slowing down; it’s a supply constraint. May carried an intense marketing push plus merge freezes around Innovation Week and O11yCon SF. June rebounded as soon as the freezes lifted, and peak-day merges hit 70 again on June 18, matching and sustaining April’s peak.

sentences.en: On that basis, weekday-average merges went 38.0 in March, 47.9 in April, then dipped to 36.0 in May before climbing back to 41.9 across the four complete weeks of June.
sentences.zh: 在此基础上，3 月份工作日平均合并次数为 38.0，4 月份为 47.9，然后在 5 月份下降至 36.0，然后在 6 月份的整四周内回升至 41.9。

sentences.en: The May dip isn’t engineers slowing down; it’s a supply constraint.
sentences.zh: 五月份的下滑并不是工程师放慢脚步，而是工程师放慢了脚步。这是供应限制。

sentences.en: May carried an intense marketing push plus merge freezes around Innovation Week and O11yCon SF.
sentences.zh: 5 月份围绕创新周和 O11yCon SF 进行了激烈的营销推广和合并冻结。

sentences.en: June rebounded as soon as the freezes lifted, and peak-day merges hit 70 again on June 18, matching and sustaining April’s peak.
sentences.zh: 冰冻解除后，6 月份的销量立即反弹，6 月 18 日高峰日合并数量再次达到 70 辆，与 4 月份的峰值持平并维持不变。

blocks.html_en: The more interesting news isn’t the wobble in the average. It’s that a third category of work showed up entirely.

sentences.en: The more interesting news isn’t the wobble in the average.
sentences.zh: 更有趣的消息并不是平均值的波动。

sentences.en: It’s that a third category of work showed up entirely.
sentences.zh: 这是第三类工作完全出现了。

blocks.html_en: honeycomb-autobot[bot] landed its first commit on main on April 23. It’s Claude Code on AgentCore, dispatched from RWX (the same CI substrate from earlier in this post) and traced by Honeycomb, triggered from a Linear issue or an @honeycomb-autobot mention on a review, with no human anywhere in the commit-generation loop, only the review loop. That’s categorically different from “AI-assisted coding.” Human-in-loop coding still means a person is driving the session and choosing what to commit. The autobot doesn’t have anyone in that seat at all, but instead back-loads the work onto the review cycle where work is more mechanical and a human feels confident going hands-free during the actual coding.

sentences.en: honeycomb-autobot[bot] landed its first commit on main on April 23.
sentences.zh: honeycomb-autobot[bot] 于 4 月 23 日在 main 上首次提交。

sentences.en: It’s Claude Code on AgentCore, dispatched from RWX (the same CI substrate from earlier in this post) and traced by Honeycomb, triggered from a Linear issue or an @honeycomb-autobot mention on a review, with no human anywhere in the commit-generation loop, only the review loop.
sentences.zh: 这是 AgentCore 上的 Claude 代码，从 RWX（与本文前面的 CI 底层相同）发送并由 Honeycomb 跟踪，由线性问题或评论中提及的 @honeycomb-autobot 触发，提交生成循环中没有任何人，只有评论循环。

sentences.en: That’s categorically different from “AI-assisted coding.”
sentences.zh: 这与“人工智能辅助编码”截然不同。

sentences.en: Human-in-loop coding still means a person is driving the session and choosing what to commit.
sentences.zh: 人机循环编码仍然意味着一个人正在推动会话并选择要提交的内容。

sentences.en: The autobot doesn’t have anyone in that seat at all, but instead back-loads the work onto the review cycle where work is more mechanical and a human feels confident going hands-free during the actual coding.
sentences.zh: 汽车人根本没有任何人坐在那个座位上，而是将工作重新加载到审查周期中，其中工作更加机械化，并且人类在实际编码过程中可以放心地解放双手。

blocks.html_en: Three months of data on it: 3 autonomous merges in April (0.3% of the month), 13 in May (1.7%), 70 across the four weeks of June (8.4%). Over the same window, human-authored merges with zero AI attribution kept shrinking, down to 211 in four weeks of June against a 2025 baseline in the 400s a month, while total throughput held at roughly twice the 2025 baseline the whole time. Same pattern as everywhere else in this post: substitution, not addition.

sentences.en: Three months of data on it: 3 autonomous merges in April (0.3% of the month), 13 in May (1.7%), 70 across the four weeks of June (8.4%).
sentences.zh: 三个月的数据：4 月份有 3 起自主合并（占当月的 0.3%），5 月份有 13 起（占 1.7%），6 月份的四个星期内有 70 起（占当月的 8.4%）。

sentences.en: Over the same window, human-authored merges with zero AI attribution kept shrinking, down to 211 in four weeks of June against a 2025 baseline in the 400s a month, while total throughput held at roughly twice the 2025 baseline the whole time.
sentences.zh: 在同一时间段内，人工编写的零人工智能归因合并不断减少，在 6 月的四个星期内降至 211 起，而 2025 年基线为每月 400 起，而总吞吐量始终保持在 2025 年基线的大约两倍。

sentences.en: Same pattern as everywhere else in this post: substitution, not addition.
sentences.zh: 与本文其他地方的模式相同：替换，而不是添加。

blocks.html_en: The three-way split, January 2025 through the four full weeks of June 2026, mechanical bots excluded. The assisted band is a floor; the autonomous band is exact, because bot authorship is self-evident.

sentences.en: The three-way split, January 2025 through the four full weeks of June 2026, mechanical bots excluded.
sentences.zh: 三路划分，从 2025 年 1 月到 2026 年 6 月的整整四个星期，不包括机械机器人。

sentences.en: The assisted band is a floor; the autonomous band is exact, because bot authorship is self-evident.
sentences.zh: 辅助带是地板；自治带是准确的，因为机器人作者是不言而喻的。

blocks.html_en: The adoption shape looks like the rest of the story too, not a power-user phenomenon. Of the 96 autobot squashes on main through July 6, 63 carry an explicit “Triggered by” line naming 23 distinct engineers; one of the eng enablement leads who co-authored autobot is the heaviest user at 18, and everyone else in the tail is 2 to 4 each. And 55 of those 96 squashes carry no Claude co-author trailer at all, which means the same trailer-based blind spot from the “Bee-ing honest” section up top shows up here too. If we counted the autobot’s work by trailer the way we count human-driven work, we’d have missed 57% of it. We count it by author identity instead, which for a bot account is exact rather than a floor. But it’s a reminder that every attribution method has exactly one failure mode it’s blind to, and you only find out what it is by checking, not by assuming it doesn’t have one.

sentences.en: The adoption shape looks like the rest of the story too, not a power-user phenomenon.
sentences.zh: 采用情况看起来也与故事的其余部分相似，而不是高级用户现象。

sentences.en: Of the 96 autobot squashes on main through July 6, 63 carry an explicit “Triggered by” line naming 23 distinct engineers; one of the eng enablement leads who co-authored autobot is the heaviest user at 18, and everyone else in the tail is 2 to 4 each.
sentences.zh: 截至 7 月 6 日，在主线上​​的 96 场汽车人大会中，有 63 场带有明确的“触发者”台词，点名了 23 名不同的工程师；与人共同创作《汽车人》的一位工程师支持主管是 18 岁的最重用户，而尾部的其他人每人都是 2 到 4 人。

sentences.en: And 55 of those 96 squashes carry no Claude co-author trailer at all, which means the same trailer-based blind spot from the “Bee-ing honest” section up top shows up here too.
sentences.zh: 这 96 个南瓜中有 55 个根本没有克劳德合著者的预告片，这意味着顶部“诚实的蜜蜂”部分中基于预告片的盲点也出现在这里。

sentences.en: If we counted the autobot’s work by trailer the way we count human-driven work, we’d have missed 57% of it.
sentences.zh: 如果我们用拖车来计算汽车人的工作，就像计算人类驱动的工作一样，我们就会错过 57% 的工作。

sentences.en: We count it by author identity instead, which for a bot account is exact rather than a floor.
sentences.zh: 我们按照作者身份来计算，对于机器人帐户来说，这是精确的而不是下限。

sentences.en: But it’s a reminder that every attribution method has exactly one failure mode it’s blind to, and you only find out what it is by checking, not by assuming it doesn’t have one.
sentences.zh: 但这提醒我们，每种归因方法都有一种它看不见的故障模式，你只能通过检查才能发现它是什么，而不是假设它没有这种故障模式。

blocks.html_en: The autonomous workflow rides the frontier model the same way human delegation does: 30 of 33 model-tagged autobot squashes in the four weeks of June cite Opus 4.8. And it isn’t yet a lines-of-code story. Autonomous work has added roughly 5,500 lines total since April, about 0.3% of the codebase’s current size. (Added, not surviving; it’s too early to measure how many of those lines are still alive at HEAD.) Right now this is a merge-count phenomenon, not a codebase-composition one. It’ll be worth watching whether that changes.

sentences.en: The autonomous workflow rides the frontier model the same way human delegation does: 30 of 33 model-tagged autobot squashes in the four weeks of June cite Opus 4.8.
sentences.zh: 自主工作流程采用前沿模型的方式与人类委托的方式相同：在 6 月的四个星期内，33 个带有模型标签的汽车人挤压中，有 30 个引用了 Opus 4.8。

sentences.en: And it isn’t yet a lines-of-code story.
sentences.zh: 而且这还不是一个代码行故事。

sentences.en: Autonomous work has added roughly 5,500 lines total since April, about 0.3% of the codebase’s current size.
sentences.zh: 自 4 月份以来，自治工作总共增加了大约 5,500 行代码，约占代码库当前大小的 0.3%。

sentences.en: (Added, not surviving; it’s too early to measure how many of those lines are still alive at HEAD.)
sentences.zh: （补充一下，不是幸存；现在衡量 HEAD 有多少行还活着还为时过早。）

sentences.en: Right now this is a merge-count phenomenon, not a codebase-composition one.
sentences.zh: 现在这是一种合并计数现象，而不是代码库组合现象。

sentences.en: It’ll be worth watching whether that changes.
sentences.zh: 这种情况是否会发生变化值得关注。

blocks.html_en: The codebase kept growing underneath all of this. HEAD was at 1.88 million lines in April; by July 6 it’s 2,096,286, more than double the 972,000 lines at the end of 2024. Net-new code since end-2024 is now majority AI-attributed for the first time: 599,000 of 1,124,000 added lines, at least 53%, up from 41% in April. June’s line-level floor, at least 82.6% of new lines AI-attributed, is the highest month on record, ahead of April’s 75%. The projection in the April data I presented on-stage, that AI would cross a third of HEAD “around end of 2026,” turned out to be conservative; the current slope puts that closer to September, with half of HEAD by roughly mid-2027.

sentences.en: The codebase kept growing underneath all of this.
sentences.zh: 在这一切的推动下，代码库不断增长。

sentences.en: HEAD was at 1.88 million lines in April; by July 6 it’s 2,096,286, more than double the 972,000 lines at the end of 2024.
sentences.zh: 4 月份 HEAD 行数为 188 万行；截至 7 月 6 日，线路数量已达 2,096,286 条，是 2024 年底 972,000 条线路的两倍多。

sentences.en: Net-new code since end-2024 is now majority AI-attributed for the first time: 599,000 of 1,124,000 added lines, at least 53%, up from 41% in April.
sentences.zh: 自 2024 年底以来的净新代码首次大部分由 AI 贡献：添加的 1,124,000 行中有 599,000 行，至少占 53%，高于 4 月份的 41%。

sentences.en: June’s line-level floor, at least 82.6% of new lines AI-attributed, is the highest month on record, ahead of April’s 75%.
sentences.zh: 6 月份的线路水平下限，至少 82.6% 的新线路由人工智能贡献，是有记录以来最高的月份，高于 4 月份的 75%。

sentences.en: The projection in the April data I presented on-stage, that AI would cross a third of HEAD “around end of 2026,” turned out to be conservative; the current slope puts that closer to September, with half of HEAD by roughly mid-2027.
sentences.zh: 我在台上展示的 4 月份数据中的预测是，人工智能将在“2026 年底左右”占据 HEAD 的三分之一，结果证明这是保守的；目前的斜率使该时间更接近 9 月，大约到 2027 年中期，HEAD 将达到一半。

blocks.html_en: One number needs its own caveat rather than a triumphant read. Human-attributed lines surviving at HEAD actually ticked down slightly, from 1,509,000 in April to 1,497,000 in July. That’s not a clean “AI replaced human code” story. Some of it is genuine replacement; some of it is recalibration retroactively reclassifying PRs that were originally counted as human, as we keep finding hidden AI attribution in old PRs. Don’t read a precise story into that number. Read it as more evidence that the floor keeps rising as we get better at measuring it, which has been true of every number in this post so far.

sentences.en: One number needs its own caveat rather than a triumphant read.
sentences.zh: 一个数字需要自己的警告，而不是胜利的阅读。

sentences.en: Human-attributed lines surviving at HEAD actually ticked down slightly, from 1,509,000 in April to 1,497,000 in July.
sentences.zh: HEAD 中幸存的人类归因品系实际上略有下降，从 4 月份的 1,509,000 个减少到 7 月份的 1,497,000 个。

sentences.en: That’s not a clean “AI replaced human code” story.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个干净的“人工智能取代人类代码”的故事。

sentences.en: Some of it is genuine replacement; some of it is recalibration retroactively reclassifying PRs that were originally counted as human, as we keep finding hidden AI attribution in old PRs.
sentences.zh: 有些是正品替换品；其中一些是重新校准，对最初被视为人类的 PR 进行追溯性重新分类，因为我们不断在旧 PR 中发现隐藏的 AI 归因。

sentences.en: Don’t read a precise story into that number.
sentences.zh: 不要将这个数字解读为一个精确的故事。

sentences.en: Read it as more evidence that the floor keeps rising as we get better at measuring it, which has been true of every number in this post so far.
sentences.zh: 将其视为更多证据，表明随着我们更好地衡量它，下限不断上升，到目前为止，这篇文章中的每个数字都是如此。

blocks.html_en: Engineers with at least one AI-attributed commit: 70, up from 64 in April. That’s broadening, not just the same 50-plus people going faster. And the frontier-model succession kept stair-stepping exactly the way it did in February: Opus 4.6 gave way to Opus 4.7, which gave way to Opus 4.8 (366 of June’s model-tagged PRs, against 31 for 4.7 and 29 for 4.6, the same one-month displacement pattern each time). Claude Fable 5, the newest Mythos-tier model, shows up in June’s trailers too, on 34 PRs. Sonnet 5 hasn’t landed a merged PR yet, since it only just launched, but it’s already showing up on PRs out for review, which tends to be the leading indicator before it shows up in this table. And the autonomous workflow doesn’t relax the one constraint that’s held the whole way through this post: a person still has to trigger it, via a Linear ticket or an @-mention. Our human names have stopped showing up on the commit messages. The only adoption ceiling is still how many engineers choose to reach for it.

sentences.en: Engineers with at least one AI-attributed commit: 70, up from 64 in April.
sentences.zh: 至少做出一项 AI 贡献的工程师：70 名，高于 4 月份的 64 名。

sentences.en: That’s broadening, not just the same 50-plus people going faster.
sentences.zh: 这正在扩大，而不仅仅是同样的 50 多人走得更快。

sentences.en: And the frontier-model succession kept stair-stepping exactly the way it did in February: Opus 4.6 gave way to Opus 4.7, which gave way to Opus 4.8 (366 of June’s model-tagged PRs, against 31 for 4.7 and 29 for 4.6, the same one-month displacement pattern each time).
sentences.zh: 前沿模型的继承与 2 月份的情况一模一样：Opus 4.6 让位于 Opus 4.7，Opus 4.7 让位于 Opus 4.8（6 月份的模型标记 PR 中有 366 个，4.7 有 31 个，4.6 有 29 个，每次都相同的一个月位移模式）。

sentences.en: Claude Fable 5, the newest Mythos-tier model, shows up in June’s trailers too, on 34 PRs.
sentences.zh: 《克劳德寓言 5》是最新的神话级模型，也出现在 6 月份的预告片中，共有 34 个 PR。

sentences.en: Sonnet 5 hasn’t landed a merged PR yet, since it only just launched, but it’s already showing up on PRs out for review, which tends to be the leading indicator before it shows up in this table.
sentences.zh: Sonnet 5 尚未获得合并的 PR，因为它刚刚推出，但它已经出现在供审查的 PR 中，这往往是它出现在此表中之前的领先指标。

sentences.en: And the autonomous workflow doesn’t relax the one constraint that’s held the whole way through this post: a person still has to trigger it, via a Linear ticket or an @-mention.
sentences.zh: 自主工作流程并没有放松贯穿本文的一个约束：人们仍然必须通过线性票证或@提及来触发它。

sentences.en: Our human names have stopped showing up on the commit messages.
sentences.zh: 我们的人名已不再出现在提交消息中。

sentences.en: The only adoption ceiling is still how many engineers choose to reach for it.
sentences.zh: 唯一的采用上限仍然是有多少工程师选择达到它。

blocks.html_en: The succession, extended through June. Each Opus release displaces its predecessor in committed work within about a month of arriving; the February pattern wasn’t a one-off.

sentences.en: The succession, extended through June.
sentences.zh: 继任期延长至六月。

sentences.en: Each Opus release displaces its predecessor in committed work within about a month of arriving; the February pattern wasn’t a one-off.
sentences.zh: 每个 Opus 版本都会在发布后大约一个月内取代其前身的承诺工作；二月的模式并不是一次性的。

blocks.html_en: Possible (overlapping) explanations

sentences.en: Possible (overlapping) explanations
sentences.zh: 可能的（重叠的）解释

blocks.html_en: We can name several factors that line up with the inflection. None of them, on its own, explains the curve, and we can’t isolate which mattered most without a controlled test we can’t run in retrospect. What follows is a list of overlapping contributions our own team has flagged, not one single cause dressed up as several.

sentences.en: We can name several factors that line up with the inflection.
sentences.zh: 我们可以说出几个与拐点相符的因素。

sentences.en: None of them, on its own, explains the curve, and we can’t isolate which mattered most without a controlled test we can’t run in retrospect.
sentences.zh: 它们本身都无法解释这条曲线，而且如果没有我们无法回顾的受控测试，我们就无法分离出最重要的因素。

sentences.en: What follows is a list of overlapping contributions our own team has flagged, not one single cause dressed up as several.
sentences.zh: 以下是我们自己的团队标记的重叠贡献的列表，没有一个单一的事业伪装成多个事业。

blocks.html_en: Frontier-model capability: Opus 4.6, in February 2026, was the specific release that produced the session-rate jump. Earlier Opus releases (4.1 in August 2025, 4.5 in December) shifted the floor without producing a takeoff. The rest of the model family in the same window, Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5, didn’t move the committable-delegation needle in our telemetry: Sonnet 4.6 launched February 17 with full Honeycomb adoption (41 distinct users) but produced only 35 commits in March and 27 in April, against Opus 4.6’s 232 and 127. It was frontier-model capability specifically that crossed the threshold from consultation to delegation, not “any new model.”

sentences.en: Frontier-model capability: Opus 4.6, in February 2026, was the specific release that produced the session-rate jump.
sentences.zh: 前沿模型功能：2026 年 2 月发布的 Opus 4.6 是导致会话率跃升的特定版本。

sentences.en: Earlier Opus releases (4.1 in August 2025, 4.5 in December) shifted the floor without producing a takeoff.
sentences.zh: 早期的 Opus 版本（2025 年 8 月发布 4.1，12 月发布 4.5）取得了一定进展，但并未取得成功。

sentences.en: The rest of the model family in the same window, Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5, didn’t move the committable-delegation needle in our telemetry: Sonnet 4.6 launched February 17 with full Honeycomb adoption (41 distinct users) but produced only 35 commits in March and 27 in April, against Opus 4.6’s 232 and 127.
sentences.zh: 同一窗口中的模型系列的其余部分，Sonnet 4.6 和 Haiku 4.5，在我们的遥测中没有移动可提交委托指针：Sonnet 4.6 于 2 月 17 日推出，并得到了完整的 Honeycomb 采用（41 个不同的用户），但在 3 月和 4 月仅产生了 35 次提交，而 Opus 4.6 则为 232 次和 127 次。

sentences.en: It was frontier-model capability specifically that crossed the threshold from consultation to delegation, not “any new model.”
sentences.zh: 跨越从协商到授权的门槛的是前沿模式能力，而不是“任何新模式”。

blocks.html_en: Leadership signaling, twice: August 2025’s letter set the explicit “experiment, take time to figure out what AI does for your work” frame. January 2026’s follow-up named sharper strategic stakes: rebuild the product surface and the market posture for AI-first. The mid-January realignment toward greenfield, higher-AI-leverage work followed from that. Without either signal, I doubt the throughput curve looks the same.

sentences.en: Leadership signaling, twice: August 2025’s letter set the explicit “experiment, take time to figure out what AI does for your work” frame.
sentences.zh: 领导层发出两次信号：2025 年 8 月的信函设定了明确的“实验，花时间弄清楚人工智能对你的工作有何作用”的框架。

sentences.en: January 2026’s follow-up named sharper strategic stakes: rebuild the product surface and the market posture for AI-first.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 1 月的后续行动命名为更明确的战略赌注：重建产品表面和人工智能优先的市场态势。

sentences.en: The mid-January realignment toward greenfield, higher-AI-leverage work followed from that.
sentences.zh: 随后，一月中旬的调整转向了绿地、更高人工智能杠杆的工作。

sentences.en: Without either signal, I doubt the throughput curve looks the same.
sentences.zh: 如果没有任何信号，我怀疑吞吐量曲线看起来是否相同。

blocks.html_en: Tooling that improved over months: The October 2025 Claude Code harness improvements produced a noticeable adoption step on their own, with no model release behind them. Each release of the tooling added something some engineer needed before they could delegate. Models alone don’t explain the curve; the wrapper around the model matters just as much as the model does.

sentences.en: Tooling that improved over months: The October 2025 Claude Code harness improvements produced a noticeable adoption step on their own, with no model release behind them.
sentences.zh: 工具经过数月的改进：2025 年 10 月的 Claude Code 工具改进本身就产生了显着的采用步骤，背后没有任何模型发布。

sentences.en: Each release of the tooling added something some engineer needed before they could delegate.
sentences.zh: 每个版本的工具都添加了一些工程师在授权之前需要的东西。

sentences.en: Models alone don’t explain the curve; the wrapper around the model matters just as much as the model does.
sentences.zh: 模型本身并不能解释曲线；模型本身并不能解释曲线。模型的包装器与模型一样重要。

blocks.html_en: Substrate already in place: Continuous delivery, code-ownership practices, fast CI, blameless incident analysis, observability that links shipped code back to the PR that created it: the practices the rest of this post is about. AI work landed in an org that already had the substrate to absorb it. We can’t run the counterfactual, but the practices section below is our best account of why this didn’t go badly.

sentences.en: Substrate already in place: Continuous delivery, code-ownership practices, fast CI, blameless incident analysis, observability that links shipped code back to the PR that created it: the practices the rest of this post is about.
sentences.zh: 基础已经就位：持续交付、代码所有权实践、快速 CI、无过错事件分析、将交付的代码链接回创建它的 PR 的可观察性：本文其余部分将讨论这些实践。

sentences.en: AI work landed in an org that already had the substrate to absorb it.
sentences.zh: 人工智能工作落地的组织已经具备了吸收它的基础。

sentences.en: We can’t run the counterfactual, but the practices section below is our best account of why this didn’t go badly.
sentences.zh: 我们无法运行反事实，但下面的实践部分是我们最好的解释，说明为什么这并没有那么糟糕。

blocks.html_en: Cumulative engineer-level expertise: From April through September 2025, one or two engineers a month adopted Claude Code. By February 2026 we had a base of fifty-plus engineers who’d been using it for months and could mentor everyone else. That cohort effect is hard to pin to any single date; it’s the gradual accumulation of in-house expertise that the February takeoff drew on.

sentences.en: Cumulative engineer-level expertise: From April through September 2025, one or two engineers a month adopted Claude Code.
sentences.zh: 累积工程师级专业知识：从 2025 年 4 月到 2025 年 9 月，每月有一到两名工程师采用 Claude Code。

sentences.en: By February 2026 we had a base of fifty-plus engineers who’d been using it for months and could mentor everyone else.
sentences.zh: 到 2026 年 2 月，我们已经拥有了 50 多名工程师，他们已经使用它几个月了，可以指导其他人。

sentences.en: That cohort effect is hard to pin to any single date; it’s the gradual accumulation of in-house expertise that the February takeoff drew on.
sentences.zh: 这种群体效应很难固定在任何一个日期。二月份的腾飞得益于内部专业知识的逐渐积累。

blocks.html_en: These factors overlap, and we can’t say any one of them in isolation would have gotten us here. Leadership signaling probably amplifies tooling improvements. Substrate makes it possible for engineers to share what they’re learning. Frontier-model capability matters more in an org that already has the substrate and the cohort to use it well. The factors compound rather than substitute for one another. Anyone telling you a single factor caused their team’s gain is overstating it. Including us.

sentences.en: These factors overlap, and we can’t say any one of them in isolation would have gotten us here.
sentences.zh: 这些因素相互重叠，我们不能说孤立地看其中任何一个因素都会导致我们走到这一步。

sentences.en: Leadership signaling probably amplifies tooling improvements.
sentences.zh: 领导力信号可能会放大工具的改进。

sentences.en: Substrate makes it possible for engineers to share what they’re learning.
sentences.zh: Substrate 使工程师能够分享他们所学到的知识。

sentences.en: Frontier-model capability matters more in an org that already has the substrate and the cohort to use it well.
sentences.zh: 对于已经拥有基础和能够很好地使用它的群体的组织来说，前沿模型能力更为重要。

sentences.en: The factors compound rather than substitute for one another.
sentences.zh: 这些因素是复合的，而不是相互替代的。

sentences.en: Anyone telling you a single factor caused their team’s gain is overstating it.
sentences.zh: 任何人告诉你一个单一因素导致了他们团队的进步都是夸大其词。

sentences.en: Including us.
sentences.zh: 包括我们。

blocks.html_en: We followed Intercom, with caveats

sentences.en: We followed Intercom, with caveats
sentences.zh: 我们遵循对讲机，但有注意事项

blocks.html_en: Three things are worth crediting in Intercom's playbook.

sentences.en: Three things are worth crediting in Intercom's playbook.
sentences.zh: Intercom 的策略中有三件事值得赞扬。

blocks.html_en: They set a more realistic and achievable 2x goal, not the 10x-and-up claims that were common currency during the 2024 hype cycle. Discipline matters when the temptation is to oversell what AI delivers. We wanted some of that discipline for ourselves.

sentences.en: They set a more realistic and achievable 2x goal, not the 10x-and-up claims that were common currency during the 2024 hype cycle.
sentences.zh: 他们设定了一个更现实、更容易实现的 2 倍目标，而不是 2024 年炒作周期中常见的 10 倍及以上的目标。

sentences.en: Discipline matters when the temptation is to oversell what AI delivers.
sentences.zh: 当人们试图过度推销人工智能所提供的功能时，纪律就很重要。

sentences.en: We wanted some of that discipline for ourselves.
sentences.zh: 我们希望自己也能遵守一些纪律。

blocks.html_en: They were transparent about both the methodology and the org-shape changes that came with the gain. The substrate Intercom built, a Claude Code plugin marketplace with 153 contributors representing 31% of their R&D org and 267 skills, is genuinely platform infrastructure that ships its own product. They spun up a dedicated team, team-2x, to build it. We’re smaller and a couple of years younger, but we’re building toward something in the same shape, at our scale.

sentences.en: They were transparent about both the methodology and the org-shape changes that came with the gain.
sentences.zh: 他们对方法论和随之而来的组织结构变化都是透明的。

sentences.en: The substrate Intercom built, a Claude Code plugin marketplace with 153 contributors representing 31% of their R&D org and 267 skills, is genuinely platform infrastructure that ships its own product.
sentences.zh: Intercom 构建的底层是一个 Claude Code 插件市场，拥有 153 名贡献者（占其研发组织的 31%）和 267 项技能，是真正的平台基础设施，可以提供自己的产品。

sentences.en: They spun up a dedicated team, team-2x, to build it.
sentences.zh: 他们组建了一个专门的团队 team-2x 来构建它。

sentences.en: We’re smaller and a couple of years younger, but we’re building toward something in the same shape, at our scale.
sentences.zh: 我们的规模较小，而且年轻了几岁，但我们正在以我们的规模朝着同样的方向发展。

blocks.html_en: They engaged their auditors, Schellman, early, before scaling auto-approval, to confirm that the evidence trail an AI-approved PR produces is the same evidence trail an auditor expects from a human-approved one. The “who” changes. The “what” doesn’t. That’s a model worth following: build for safety first, and compliance follows from it.

sentences.en: They engaged their auditors, Schellman, early, before scaling auto-approval, to confirm that the evidence trail an AI-approved PR produces is the same evidence trail an auditor expects from a human-approved one.
sentences.zh: 在扩大自动批准范围之前，他们很早就与审计员 Schellman 进行了接触，以确认人工智能批准的 PR 产生的证据线索与审计员期望从人类批准的证据线索中得到的证据线索相同。

sentences.en: The “who” changes.
sentences.zh: “谁”发生了变化。

sentences.en: The “what” doesn’t.
sentences.zh: 而“什么”则不然。

sentences.en: That’s a model worth following: build for safety first, and compliance follows from it.
sentences.zh: 这是一个值得遵循的模型：安全第一，合规性随之而来。

blocks.html_en: Where we differ is that we’re at zero auto-approval today, and they’re at 19.2% as of April. That’s deliberate sequencing on our part, not a sign that we're “behind.” Before you can safely scale auto-approval, which is automating the bug-catching half of PR review, you need substantial substrate underneath it. The compliance question is necessary but not sufficient; the technical preconditions sit underneath the procedural ones. You need codified rules in CLAUDE.md and skills that an auto-review agent can actually verify against; MCP-mediated dev-loop access so the agent reviews against the same context a human would have had (design intent, ticket history, production behavior); fast and AI-legible CI so the verification loop closes quickly; closed-loop production observability that links shipped code back to the PR that created it; and a dissemination layer so humans stay aware of what’s shipping even when they’re not gating it.

sentences.en: Where we differ is that we’re at zero auto-approval today, and they’re at 19.2% as of April.
sentences.zh: 我们的不同之处在于，我们今天的自动批准率为零，截至 4 月份为 19.2%。

sentences.en: That’s deliberate sequencing on our part, not a sign that we're “behind.”
sentences.zh: 这是我们故意的排序，而不是我们“落后”的迹象。

sentences.en: Before you can safely scale auto-approval, which is automating the bug-catching half of PR review, you need substantial substrate underneath it.
sentences.zh: 在您能够安全地扩展自动批准（自动批准 PR 审查中的 bug 捕获部分）之前，您需要在其背后提供大量的基础。

sentences.en: The compliance question is necessary but not sufficient; the technical preconditions sit underneath the procedural ones.
sentences.zh: 合规性问题是必要的，但还不够；技术前提条件位于程序前提条件之下。

sentences.en: You need codified rules in CLAUDE.md and skills that an auto-review agent can actually verify against; MCP-mediated dev-loop access so the agent reviews against the same context a human would have had (design intent, ticket history, production behavior); fast and AI-legible CI so the verification loop closes quickly; closed-loop production observability that links shipped code back to the PR that created it; and a dissemination layer so humans stay aware of what’s shipping even when they’re not gating it.
sentences.zh: 您需要 CLAUDE.md 中的编纂规则以及自动审核代理可以实际验证的技能； MCP 介导的开发循环访问，因此代理可以根据人类拥有的相同上下文进行审查（设计意图、票证历史记录、生产行为）；快速且人工智能可读的 CI，因此验证循环可以快速关闭；闭环生产可观察性，将已发布的代码链接回创建它的 PR；和一个传播层，这样人们即使在没有门控的情况下也能知道正在发送的内容。

blocks.html_en: A 19% auto-approval number means radically different things at an org that invested in the substrate before turning the switch versus one that just turned the switch. Intercom’s number is downstream of substrate they built first. A company that turns on “auto-approve PRs under 20 lines” without equivalent substrate will report the same number, but it’s measuring rubber-stamping against weak constraints, not safe automation against strong ones. Different point on the same trajectory.

sentences.en: A 19% auto-approval number means radically different things at an org that invested in the substrate before turning the switch versus one that just turned the switch.
sentences.zh: 19% 的自动批准率对于在转向之前投资基质的组织与刚刚转向的组织来说意味着完全不同的事情。

sentences.en: Intercom’s number is downstream of substrate they built first.
sentences.zh: 对讲机的号码位于他们首先建造的基板的下游。

sentences.en: A company that turns on “auto-approve PRs under 20 lines” without equivalent substrate will report the same number, but it’s measuring rubber-stamping against weak constraints, not safe automation against strong ones.
sentences.zh: 一家在没有同等基材的情况下打开“自动批准 20 行以下 PR”的公司将报告相同的数字，但它是针对弱约束来衡量橡皮图章，而不是针对强约束来衡量安全自动化。

sentences.en: Different point on the same trajectory.
sentences.zh: 同一轨迹上的不同点。

blocks.html_en: The headline finding from Intercom’s auto-approval post is the most striking parallel to our own data. They report AI-authored backend code reverting at 0.53% and AI-authored frontend code reverting at 0.22%, against human-authored revert rates of 5.39% and 2.00% respectively. It’s worth naming the selection effect here: if the easier, lower-risk changes increasingly get auto-approved, humans are left reviewing the harder residual cases, which would push human revert rates up for reasons that have nothing to do with humans getting worse at their jobs. Their downtime from breaking code changes dropped 35% even as deployment frequency doubled. Theirs is a per-PR claim about strict, decomposed, sub-agent-driven review against an Intercom-specific guidance flywheel. Ours is a per-quarter claim about severity, not volume: incident count is tracking change volume about as linearly as you’d expect, but we haven’t had a spectacular AI failure, and our guardrails are aimed at containing how bad any one incident gets rather than pretending we can hold the count flat. Different denominators, same direction. Both posts are pushing back on the naive “more code, more failures” intuition, from different evidence.

sentences.en: The headline finding from Intercom’s auto-approval post is the most striking parallel to our own data.
sentences.zh: Intercom 自动批准帖子的标题发现与我们自己的数据最惊人的相似之处。

sentences.en: They report AI-authored backend code reverting at 0.53% and AI-authored frontend code reverting at 0.22%, against human-authored revert rates of 5.39% and 2.00% respectively.
sentences.zh: 他们报告称，AI 编写的后端代码恢复率为 0.53%，AI 编写的前端代码恢复率为 0.22%，而人类编写的恢复率分别为 5.39% 和 2.00%。

sentences.en: It’s worth naming the selection effect here: if the easier, lower-risk changes increasingly get auto-approved, humans are left reviewing the harder residual cases, which would push human revert rates up for reasons that have nothing to do with humans getting worse at their jobs.
sentences.zh: 这里值得一提的是选择效应：如果更容易、风险更低的变更越来越多地获得自动批准，那么人类就只能审查更困难的剩余案例，这将推高人类的回复率，而其原因与人类的工作恶化无关。

sentences.en: Their downtime from breaking code changes dropped 35% even as deployment frequency doubled.
sentences.zh: 即使部署频率翻倍，他们因破坏代码更改而导致的停机时间也减少了 35%。

sentences.en: Theirs is a per-PR claim about strict, decomposed, sub-agent-driven review against an Intercom-specific guidance flywheel.
sentences.zh: 他们的PR声明是针对Intercom特定的引导飞轮进行严格的、分解的、子代理驱动的审查。

sentences.en: Ours is a per-quarter claim about severity, not volume: incident count is tracking change volume about as linearly as you’d expect, but we haven’t had a spectacular AI failure, and our guardrails are aimed at containing how bad any one incident gets rather than pretending we can hold the count flat.
sentences.zh: 我们的声明是按季度计算的严重性，而不是数量：事件计数与您预期的一样线性地跟踪变化量，但我们还没有遇到严重的人工智能故障，我们的护栏旨在控制任何一个事件的严重程度，而不是假装我们可以保持计数不变。

sentences.en: Different denominators, same direction.
sentences.zh: 分母不同，方向相同。

sentences.en: Both posts are pushing back on the naive “more code, more failures” intuition, from different evidence.
sentences.zh: 这两篇文章都从不同的证据中反驳了天真的“代码越多，失败越多”的直觉。

blocks.html_en: We had a private conversation with the Intercom team in early May 2026. They hit the same February 2026 inflection point we did. The Opus 4.6 unlock was the gut-call attribution from multiple Intercom engineers, though they noted it was almost impossible to disentangle from their internal mandates and team-2x activity in the same period. They’re partnering with a Stanford research group to try to isolate the variables, and even that group’s initial pre-January analysis missed the inflection entirely. The world’s most data-rich org on this exact question is paying academic researchers to help them figure it out, and they still don’t know.

sentences.en: We had a private conversation with the Intercom team in early May 2026.
sentences.zh: 我们于 2026 年 5 月上旬与 Intercom 团队进行了一次私人谈话。

sentences.en: They hit the same February 2026 inflection point we did.
sentences.zh: 他们在 2026 年 2 月遇到了与我们相同的拐点。

sentences.en: The Opus 4.6 unlock was the gut-call attribution from multiple Intercom engineers, though they noted it was almost impossible to disentangle from their internal mandates and team-2x activity in the same period.
sentences.zh: Opus 4.6 的解锁是多位 Intercom 工程师的直觉归因，尽管他们指出，几乎不可能摆脱他们的内部任务和同一时期的 team-2x 活动。

sentences.en: They’re partnering with a Stanford research group to try to isolate the variables, and even that group’s initial pre-January analysis missed the inflection entirely.
sentences.zh: 他们正在与斯坦福大学的一个研究小组合作，试图隔离变量，甚至该小组一月份前的初步分析也完全错过了这种变化。

sentences.en: The world’s most data-rich org on this exact question is paying academic researchers to help them figure it out, and they still don’t know.
sentences.zh: 关于这个问题，世界上数据最丰富的组织正在付钱给学术研究人员来帮助他们解决这个问题，但他们仍然不知道。

blocks.html_en: That independent-org corroboration is the cleanest natural experiment either of us has. Two separate orgs, same month, same uncertainty about cause, same direction. That’s worth more than either of our individual analyses on its own. It’s also worth weighing against the broader base rate: DX’s longitudinal study across roughly 400 companies found AI usage up 65% translating to only about 8% more PR throughput on average. We’re the outlier case here, not the median one.

sentences.en: That independent-org corroboration is the cleanest natural experiment either of us has.
sentences.zh: 这种独立组织的证实是我们做过的最干净的自然实验。

sentences.en: Two separate orgs, same month, same uncertainty about cause, same direction.
sentences.zh: 两个不同的组织，同一个月，同样的原因不确定性，同样的方向。

sentences.en: That’s worth more than either of our individual analyses on its own.
sentences.zh: 这比我们个人的任何分析本身都更有价值。

sentences.en: It’s also worth weighing against the broader base rate: DX’s longitudinal study across roughly 400 companies found AI usage up 65% translating to only about 8% more PR throughput on average.
sentences.zh: 它还值得权衡更广泛的基本比率：DX 对大约 400 家公司的纵向研究发现，人工智能的使用率上升了 65%，平均而言，公关吞吐量仅增加了约 8%。

sentences.en: We’re the outlier case here, not the median one.
sentences.zh: 我们是这里的异常情况，而不是中值情况。

blocks.html_en: Here's the truth: Nothing I've written here will help you if your underlying org isn't already healthy and functional. AI just amplifies what you're already doing. Read part 2 of this blog series to see what we learned.

sentences.en: Here's the truth: Nothing I've written here will help you if your underlying org isn't already healthy and functional.
sentences.zh: 事实是这样的：如果您的底层组织尚未健康运转，那么我在这里写的任何内容都无法帮助您。

sentences.en: AI just amplifies what you're already doing.
sentences.zh: 人工智能只是放大你已经在做的事情。

sentences.en: Read part 2 of this blog series to see what we learned.
sentences.zh: 阅读本博客系列的第 2 部分，了解我们学到了什么。
